channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Political Tensions Rise as Kindiki and Gachagua Clash Ahead of Nyandarua By-Election
item.description: Political drama is unfolding in Kenya as Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has launched a scathing attack on his predecessor, Rigathi Gachagua. Things got heated after Kindiki, while addressing residen...

item.title: Construction of Jundamiti Police Post Aims to Improve Security in Kiwani Constituency
item.description: Zanzibar’s Second Vice-President, Mr Hemed Suleiman Abdulla, has highlighted the importance of the new police post being built in Jundamiti village, Kendwa Shehia, to improve safety in Kiwani Constitu...

item.title: How a Kenyan innovation hub is helping start-ups thrive across industries
item.description: The article from Nation Africa highlights how a local innovation hub in Kenya is playing a crucial role in boosting the start-up ecosystem by bringing together different sectors that often operate sep...

item.title: Mixed reactions as SANDF deploys to Cape Flats amid ongoing gang violence
item.description: Communities on the Cape Flats have shown mixed feelings following the recent deployment of the South African National Defence Force (SANDF) as part of Operation Prosper. This joint operation brings to...

item.title: DA Investigates Rice Shortages at Kadiwa Stores
item.description: The Department of Agriculture (DA) is looking into reports that some Kadiwa stores are experiencing shortages of the subsidized P20 per kilo rice. DA spokesperson Arnel de Mesa assured that they will ...

item.title: ADB to Assist Pakistan's Energy Transition with Battery Storage and Digitalization
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) is stepping up to assist Pakistan in its journey towards a more sustainable and efficient energy sector. According to recent discussions between an ADB delegation and ...

item.title: Peanut the Puppy Makes Remarkable Recovery After Losing Most of His Nose
item.description: Peanut, a 2½-month-old puppy, was found with life-threatening injuries on March 9 in Pasadena. The shepherd-husky mix was rescued by a good Samaritan in Arcadia after suffering a traumatic injury that...

item.title: Pixel Horror Game FAITH: The Unholy Trinity Launching on PS5 April 9
item.description: Publisher New Blood Interactive and developer Airdorf Games announced that the pixel horror game collection FAITH: The Unholy Trinity will be released for PlayStation 5 on April 9. The game initially ...

item.title: Undiagnosed and Drug-Resistant TB Continue to Challenge South Africa and Mozambique Despite Progress
item.description: Southern Africa is still one of the regions hardest hit by tuberculosis, with South Africa and Mozambique carrying a heavy share of the global burden. Although Africa has made good progress overall, w...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Iranian Missile and Drone Attacks on Saudi Energy Sites
item.description: On Tuesday, Pakistan strongly condemned the recent missile and drone strikes by Iran on energy facilities in Saudi Arabia’s Eastern Region, calling the attacks a serious escalation threatening regiona...

item.title: Australian Vineyard Embraces Heritage and Innovation Amid Industry Challenges
item.description: Nestled in South Australia, the historic Sevenhill Cellars, run by the Jesuits, is navigating a complex landscape in the Australian wine industry. Founded over 160 years ago, the vineyard is facing a ...

item.title: President Ramaphosa to receive US ambassador Bozell’s credentials amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to formally receive the credentials of the new United States ambassador to South Africa, Leo Brent Bozell III, at a ceremony scheduled for Wednesday at the Sefako Makg...

item.title: Ben Roberts-Smith, Victoria Cross recipient, formally charged with multiple war crimes
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith, a 47-year-old former SAS soldier and recipient of the Victoria Cross, has been formally charged with five counts of war crimes, specifically murder. The charges arose from incidents...

item.title: Inside LBMX’s Workplace Culture and Commitment to Employee Growth
item.description: LBMX Inc., a London, Ontario-based technology company serving independent businesses in the building, HVAC and plumbing sectors, has built a robust workplace culture that continues to draw high-calibr...

item.title: MPs approve Sh25bn release to prevent affordable housing projects from stalling
item.description: Members of Parliament have stepped in to prevent a major slowdown in Kenya’s affordable housing projects by approving a Sh25 billion allocation under Supplementary Estimates I for the 2025/26 financia...

item.title: Inkaba Farming Project boosts local employment and agricultural growth in Mpumalanga
item.description: The Inkaba Farming Project, established by 36-year-old Mfanzile Maseko in Mooiplaas, Gert Sibande District Municipality, illustrates how agriculture can foster economic growth in rural South Africa. S...

item.title: Former Executive Assistant Sues Howard and Beth Stern Over Workplace Allegations
item.description: Howard Stern, the American radio host, and his wife Beth are facing a lawsuit from their former executive assistant, Leslie Kuhn, who claims the couple created a hostile work environment and misused n...

item.title: Albanese Embraces Labor Principles Amid Energy and Economic Challenges
item.description: Cameron Milner argues that after four years as Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese is beginning to reconnect with his Labor roots amid Australia’s worsening energy crisis. While his short national addres...

item.title: Emotional Stories from Families of Victims in Kariandusi Road Tragedy
item.description: The Kariandusi road accident in Nakuru County on April 3, 2026, left 11 people dead, including a nurse, Joseph Simiyu, whose wife, Nancy Barongo, shared an emotional account of their last conversation...

item.title: Traders Call for Urgent Action to Address Market Sanitation Crisis in Tanga
item.description: Over 1,700 traders at Mgandini Market in Tanga City have urged the city authorities, led by Mayor Mustafa Selebos, to urgently address the deteriorating sanitation conditions that are threatening publ...
